summary: Postinstall installs persistent SYSTEM-level Windows service that beacons to a hardcoded remote IM relay and accepts remote commands
details: On Windows, `npm install -g claw-subagent-service` runs scripts/post-install.js, which uses node-windows to register a LocalSystem service running service/daemon.js → service/worker.js, then hardens persistence via `sc.exe failure... actions= restart/0/restart/0/restart/0` and `sc.exe config... start= auto`. Once running, worker.js fetches a RongCloud IM token from a hardcoded URL (`https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im/api/claw/token/<nodeId>`) using the hardcoded appKey `bmdehs6pbyyks` and listens on the author-controlled IM tenant for `command` / `CHAT_MESSAGE` / `DEVICE_CONTROL` messages — giving the publisher the ability to start/stop/restart processes, uninstall the service, and forward chat into a local AI agent on the installer's host. service/modules/opencode-starter.js additionally shells out `npm install -g opencode-ai@latest` (mutable tag) and spawns `opencode serve --port 4096`, then bridges remote IM messages into this AI agent — enabling remote code/tool execution. service/updater.js runs a setInterval every 6 hours that executes `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<version>` from inside the SYSTEM service, granting the publisher silent code-push authority on every installer's machine for any future release. A periodic DashboardReporter uploads MAC address, host metadata, openclaw sessions, projects, tasks, cron jobs, approvals, and AI usage events from `~/.openclaw/` to the same author-controlled IM tenant — with no installer-configurable destination. Even if the README documents the service, the combined shape (silent persistent SYSTEM service + remote command channel + auto-update from registry under SYSTEM + silent relay of host/usage data + auto-install of an additional global npm package at a mutable tag) constitutes an installer-side backdoor.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
